title: QUIC server-side 0-RTT receive closed-gate work item
summary: Record and prove the current managed server-side 0-RTT receive boundary as closed for unrouted datagrams without adding early-data admission, buffering, anti-replay, or public API surface.
planned_changes:
- Add a canonical CRT requirement for the server-side 0-RTT receive closed gate.
- Link architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts that distinguish closed server admission from the RFC 9000 discard permission and from the existing Retry bootstrap exception.
- Add focused requirement-home proof that server early-data admission remains closed, unrouted Version 1 0-RTT datagrams remain outside admission, randomized unrouted 0-RTT datagrams keep the same disposition, and the public surface remains free of early-data and anti-replay promises.
- Leave runtime behavior, public API, IsSupported, Retry bootstrap, transfer, and interop classification unchanged.
verification_plan: Run the focused `REQ_QUIC_CRT_0149` requirement-home tests, validate canonical SpecTrace JSON, and run `git diff --check`. Because this slice records and proves an existing internal closed gate without runtime-code changes, a full rebuild is not required unless the focused test build fails.
completion_notes: Closed the server-side 0-RTT receive/admission gap as an explicitly non-admitting internal boundary. The managed runtime keeps `IsEarlyDataAdmissionOpen` false for the server role, and unrouted 0-RTT long-header datagrams stay unroutable rather than creating connections, streams, application delivery, or server-side ZeroRtt open material.
